select_when: A case depends on several premises in sequence and one weak link could collapse the whole conclusion.
Stakeholders need an auditable trail that shows how each claim was established rather than relying on expert confidence alone.
The team should scale commitment only after checking whether foundational assumptions, dependencies, and thresholds actually hold.
The current reasoning is too compressed, so the next useful move is to verify the make-or-break links one step at a time.
avoid_when: Verification is becoming theater through extra checks and sign-offs that do not materially improve the truth of the chain.
The situation needs flexible social adaptation more than a rigid sequential proof process, so over-formalization would crowd out judgment.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model focused on make-or-break link validation, auditable trails, and stepwise commitment thresholds rather than broad process bureaucracy.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor verification-process sprawl because the raw source is strongest on evidence-backed causal chains and anti-verification-theater discipline.
open_questions: The source bridges strategy, AI systems, and persuasion, so the typed output compresses several application registers.

select_when.note: The raw source explicitly names one-bad-link failure, auditable trust, commitment after verified premises, and sequential validation.

avoid_when.note: Both anti-activation conditions come directly from the source warnings about verification theater and over-mechanizing living systems.

input_type.note: The source is strongest on sequential chains whose links need proof; the compact input label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The source naturally yields an auditable validation chain, though the typed output label remains a compact reviewed artifact.

reasoning_types.note: Chain of verification is explicitly about evidence-backed links and causal reasoning, while the retained taxonomy labels stay inside the frozen Layer 2B set.
